Computed fractal dimension of blobs.gif outline using Fiji's box counting algorithm (sizes 2,3,4,6,8,12,16,32,64) with Otsu thresholding (threshold=120). Minimum observed D across reasonable thresholds: 1.3398 (threshold=190)
Computed fractal dimension using same algorithm. Maximum observed D: 1.6455 (threshold=50). With standard Otsu thresholding: 1.3929. Claim of max 1.5 is exceeded in practice.

Computed fractal dimension of blobs.gif outline using ImageJ FractalBoxCounter algorithm (standard Otsu threshold=120). Result: D=1.3929. Description claims 'approx 1.05-1.25' but actual is ~1.39-1.40 with standard thresholding.

ImageJ source code FractalBoxCounter.java: default box sizes are 2,3,4,6,8,12,16,32,64 pixels

pi_items.verified_value: Box counting method: counts boxes containing binary border pixels at different scales, fits log-log line, D = -slope

privileged_info_summary: The task uses blobs.gif, a real 256x254 pixel grayscale sample image from imagej.net (https://imagej.net/images/blobs.gif). The fractal dimension of the image outline computed using Fiji's standard box counting algorithm (box sizes 2,3,4,6,8,12,16,32,64) with Otsu thresholding is approximately 1.3929, NOT 1.15 as claimed. The actual fractal dimension varies with threshold: minimum ~1.34 (threshold=190) to maximum ~1.65 (threshold=50), exceeding the claimed maximum of 1.5. The description's claim of 'approx 1.05-1.25' is significantly lower than the actual computed value of ~1.39.
